column1: # Rows starting with # are ignored. Do not edit the header row below. Blank optional cells apply the defaults described here.
# geofence_id: system-generated, leave EMPTY for new fences. "Download current geofences" fills it in; never type your own.
# fence_name: display name, up to 200 characters. Used to match existing fences when geofence_id is blank.
# tags: tag names separated by semicolons (not commas), or write all for every tag.
# latitude / longitude: decimal degrees, e.g. 42.4733 and -83.4021.
# radius_m: circle radius in meters, 10 to 100000. Blank means 250.
# alert_on: entry, exit, both, or none. Blank means both. Controls email alerts only; crossings are always recorded.
# still_outside_min: still-outside reminder in minutes, 30 to 20160. Blank or 0 turns it off.
# email_to / email_cc: alert addresses separated by semicolons, up to 5 each. Blank email_to turns email alerts off.
# sensitivity: instant, balanced, or cautious (or 1, 2, 3). How many location updates confirm a crossing before alerting. Blank means instant.
# active: yes or no. no pauses monitoring without deleting the fence. Blank means yes.
# delete: leave empty to keep a fence; type yes to delete it.
# Example row (delete the # at the start of the next line to try it). Only geofence_id and delete are meant to stay empty:
